title: Implicit casts should not lower precision
description: Implicit casts should not lower precisionAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode SmellsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?A narrowing conversion is an implicit conversion to a destination type that cannot represent all values from the source type.It can be a floating-point type converted to an integer type, or a type with a larger range of values converted to a type with a smaller range.Narrowing conversions can lead to a loss of information and because they are implicit, they are not always obvious.int a = 2.1f; // Noncompliant: loss of floating-point precision long double f(); double d = 0; d += f(); // Noncompliant: smaller range of valuesNarrowing conversions should be fixed by either using a destination type that can represent all the source values or by using an explici
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Implicit cast may lower precision. Consider using explicit cast or appropriate destination type to preserve precision.
Narrowing conversion detected. This may result in data loss. Use explicit cast to indicate intent.
Implicit conversion from floating-point to integer type. This will truncate the fractional part. Use explicit cast like (int) if intentional.
